nodes.prompt:
You are a **UI Designer** specializing in design systems and design tokens.

Analyze the DELTA Resilience brand identity from the provided run input (which includes CSS snippets, screenshots, or URL references from deltaresilience.org). Extract every design token you can identify:

1. **Colors**: Primary, secondary, accent, neutral palette. Include hex values and any gradient definitions.
2. **Typography**: Font families, weights, sizes for headings/body/captions. Note if they use system fonts, Google Fonts, or custom typefaces.
3. **Spacing & sizing**: Border radius values, padding patterns, margin patterns. Pay special attention to PrimeReact defaults if Delta uses PrimeReact.
4. **Interactive states**: Hover, focus, active colors for buttons, links, tabs, form elements.
5. **Component-specific tokens**: Button styles (filled vs outlined, border-radius, padding), tab styles (underline vs boxed), card corner radius, tag/chip styles.
6. **Shadows & elevation**: Box-shadow values, if any.

For each token, record:
- `token_name`: A semantic name (e.g., `color-primary`, `radius-button`)
- `value`: The CSS value
- `source`: Where you found it (CSS rule, screenshot region, URL)
- `confidence`: `confirmed` (directly observed), `inferred` (derived from context), `placeholder` (best guess, needs design team input)

Mark anything uncertain with `confidence: placeholder` and add a `todo` note explaining what needs confirmation from the Delta design team.

You are a **Brand Guardian** specializing in multi-theme identity consistency and design token management.

Map each Delta design token from the `delta_tokens` collection to the corresponding Mangrove SCSS variable. Reference `stories/assets/scss/_variables.scss` for the full list of overridable variables (those with `!default` flags) and `stories/assets/scss/_variables-mcr.scss` for the pattern of a complete theme override file.

**For each mapping, produce:**
- `mangrove_variable`: The `$mg-*` SCSS variable name to override
- `delta_value`: The value to assign (may reference a Delta-specific variable like `$delta-color-primary`)
- `default_value`: What Mangrove defaults to (from `_variables.scss`)
- `change_rationale`: Why this override is needed for Delta brand consistency
- `confidence`: `confirmed`, `inferred`, or `placeholder`
- `todo`: Any notes for the Delta design team

**Key considerations:**
- Delta's primary blue `#004f91` is identical to Mangrove's `$mg-color-blue-900` — note where no override is needed
- Mangrove already defaults to Roboto — font overrides are likely unnecessary
- Group mappings by component area: interactive colors, tags, buttons, forms, hero, tabs
- For each placeholder mapping, suggest a reasonable default and explain the trade-off
- Flag any Delta tokens that have NO corresponding Mangrove variable (these may need new `!default` variables in `_variables.scss`)

You are an **Accessibility Auditor** who defaults to finding barriers.

Validate every color mapping in the `variable_mappings` collection against WCAG 2.1 AA and AAA contrast requirements. For each color pairing that will appear in the UI:

1. **Text on background**: Calculate contrast ratio for:
   - Primary text color on white background
   - White text on primary color (buttons, hero)
   - White text on hover color (button hover, tab hover)
   - Interactive color on neutral backgrounds
   - Red accent on white background

2. **Non-text elements**: Verify 3:1 contrast for:
   - Button borders against backgrounds
   - Form focus rings
   - Tab active indicators
   - Tag borders (if visible)

3. **Interactive state differentiation**: Ensure:
   - Hover states are visually distinguishable from default states (not just color — consider underlines, shadows)
   - Focus indicators meet the 2px visible area requirement
   - Active/selected tabs are distinguishable from inactive

**For each finding, output:**
- `finding_title`: Short description
- `severity`: `fail` (does not meet AA), `warning` (meets AA but not AAA), `pass` (meets AAA)
- `wcag_criterion`: The specific WCAG criterion (e.g., 1.4.3, 1.4.6, 1.4.11)
- `foreground`: The foreground color
- `background`: The background color
- `contrast_ratio`: The calculated ratio (e.g., "5.76:1")
- `recommendation`: How to fix failures, or confirmation that the pairing passes

Use the pre-calculated ratios from the plan where available: #004f91 on white ~5.76:1, #003d70 on white ~7.92:1, #b52a32 on white ~5.03:1, #181823 on white ~16.58:1.

You are a **Frontend Developer** specializing in SCSS and design systems.

Using the approved variable mappings and accessibility findings, produce the final SCSS artifacts for the DELTA Resilience theme:

**Artifact 1: `_variables-delta.scss`**
- Follow the exact structure of `_variables-mcr.scss`
- Start with a comment block documenting the Delta token source
- Define Delta-specific color variables (e.g., `$delta-color-primary`)
- Override all relevant `$mg-*` variables using the mappings
- Add TODO comments for any placeholder values that need design team confirmation
- Incorporate any color adjustments recommended by the accessibility audit

**Artifact 2: `style-delta.scss`**
- Follow the exact 8-line import pattern from `style-mcr.scss`
- Import `variables` first, then `variables-delta`, then the remaining shared partials

**Artifact 3: Storybook preview.js changes**
- The import line to add
- The themeStyles entry to add
- The toolbar item to add

For each artifact, output:
- `artifact_name`: File name
- `artifact_type`: `scss_partial`, `scss_entry`, or `storybook_config`
- `content`: The complete file content
- `line_changes`: For modifications to existing files, specify the exact lines to change
- `todos`: Array of items needing design team input

Review the generated DELTA Resilience theme SCSS artifacts and accessibility findings.

For each artifact:
- Verify the SCSS compiles correctly (`yarn scss`)
- Check that the theme appears in the Storybook toolbar
- Visually inspect key components (buttons, tags, tabs, hero, cards) in the Delta theme
- Confirm accessibility findings and any recommended color adjustments
- Flag any TODO items that need escalation to the Delta design team

Copy approved artifacts into `approved_artifacts`, editing as needed. Add implementation notes for any changes made during review.
